verdict.note: Two harnesses, one runner, zero per-harness runner code: `spex eval matrix pi` and `spex eval matrix opencode` each drove a REAL dispatched worker through all eight rows — both now 8/8 PASS, filed under each node's own historical scenario names (deliver-steer fans out to opencode's two send scenarios), with non-matrix scenarios passed through untouched. Conclusions match the manual wave: same verdicts on every row it had measured (pi stop-gate/deliver/resume/liveness/commit-gate/close; opencode stop-gate/ask/deliver/resume/liveness/commit-gate/close). The runner also proved honest under its own failures: skip when a row was unprovoked (never a fabricated verdict), and four first-wave FAILs traced to runner/environment artifacts (boot-grace kill, review transient, parallel-run probe saturation, send at a rebinding socket) were retracted with named reasons and re-measured serially after fixing the drives.
